channel.title: Comments for page "Log of Extranormal Events"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Log of Extranormal Events"

item.title: Re: Staff Post - MAST - Open
item.content:encoded: Hmm, I see. Thanks for the crit I’ll work to get this fixed before I post again.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - MAST - Open
item.content:encoded:
I said that the post was long-winded because the entry is filled with information that isn't necessary to get the concept across (such as exact measurements of the cave) and could have been shortened to something such as

Event Description: Foundation geological audio sensors within the Veryovkina Cave began to pick up faint audio later found to resemble several male and female voices singing. Further auditory analysis matched the singing to resemble ancient Gregorian folk music, although the specific composition is unknown to modern records. Singing ceased after approximately 33 minutes.

which accomplishes a similar effect without the need for unnecessary bloat.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - MAST - Open
item.content:encoded:
Long-winded is correct, as the entry is unnecessarily loquacious, with huge chunks of descriptors that can be cut out without any huge loss in relevant details. That, combined with the spattering of SPaG errors makes it a chore to read.

With that said, it's best that entries should be as high-quality as possible before being posted, so it being technically deficient doesn't inspire any confidence in me as a reader.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - MAST - Open
item.content:encoded: As this staff post is open I would like to discuss the event regarding the cave. While I understand the SPAG errors and those can be corrected I find that regarding the point “Page is Long-winded” to be slightly more of a personal opinion of the staff member pruning than an actual issue which would cause it to become deleted. Additionally there are a wealth of articles within this page that are much longer/“long-winded” than this one but have yet to be deleted. I will be adding the event back into the log after the SPAG errors are fixed and I would like a more comprehensive explanation if you decide to delete it once more as it only received positive feedback and was not blatantly a bad event unlike others on the list.

item.title: Staff Post - MAST - Open
item.content:encoded:
The following entries were pruned from this page:

+ show block
- hide block

Event Description: Five sperm whales blew up in size, broke through the surface of the water, until they exploded in large amounts of confetti
Date of Occurrence: 12/12/2012
Location: ██████, Canada
Follow-up Actions Taken: Bodies examined and incinerated. Seeing as the event was near a foundation site, there was no need for amnestics.

The entry is a retread of a previous entry about a volcano, which executed the idea in a more interesting way.

Event Description: At approximately 19:00 hours, foundation geological audio sensors within Veryovkina Cave, Georgia known officially as the world’s deepest cave measuring at roughly 2,212 meters deep began to pick up faint audio around 2,150 meters. Audio recordings after analysis by foundation researchers indicated that several male and female voices were the sources of the previously unknown audio, the voices are thought to be singing 4th Century AD Georgian folk music previously unknown to any modern records. Singing abruptly stopped around 33 minutes later and no known occurrences have happened since.
Date of Occurrence: 8/██/201█
Location: Veryovkina Cave, Abkhazia, Georgia
Follow-up Actions taken: Limited use of Amnestics due to the depth of said site and the fact that all ongoing expeditions below 1,500 feet had been suspended because of high amounts of rainfall in the area. Audio was immediately received by foundation researchers after normal audio was disrupted and two agents have been posted until 202█ to monitor the area for any further activity.

Entry is long-winded and possesses several SPaG errors.

Event Description: A Caucasian male wearing a business suit, and carrying a brief case was seen manifesting randomly throughout the city of ██████, however no persons has shown any recollection of the event. The following day, in the city of ████████, the same event occurred, and again in ████.
Date of Occurrence: ██-██-████
Location: ██████, ████████, and ████
Follow-up Actions Taken: The Foundation confiscated all footage displaying the event occurring, and Class B Amnestics were given to witnesses.

This concept isn't very interesting and isn't very anomalous to begin with.

Event Description: A pickpocket, on the run from local police, ran into an alley and hid underneath a cardboard box. Upon entering the alley and encountering the box, the pursuers appeared to lose interest and continued their search, despite the obviousness of the pickpocket's chosen cover (no other objects of sufficient size to hide an adult were present).
Date of Occurrence: 14/12/2020
Location: San Diego, California, United States
Follow-up Actions Taken: Camera footage showing the event was confiscated under a cover story of the cameras needing maintenance. Police involved in the event were interviewed. None of them seemed to find the event unusual in any way, even when shown video of the pickpocket hiding under the box. Following interviews, those involved were amnesticized. The pickpocket has not yet been located but has classified as a Person of Interest.

Reference to external media and the event itself is barely anomalous.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: All entries added to a collaboration log are meant to be added to the end. Entries added randomly throughout the log, including to the top of the page are removed during the pruning process.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's always cool to see other people take the time to do research before writing something (I suck at doing that)

Good entry!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Can we please put a ban on individuals posting their submission at the top of the page? It ruins the history of the site and just seems incredibly selfish.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I find the Rick Astley event in New York City to be the best of them all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hey guys, first time adding something actual to the site! I wanted to make my first impression look good so I did some research around my event just so it could feel a little more believable I guess. I did my event based around the actual Veryovkina cave where cave expeditions have actually heard strange noises nearby inside. I Hope to Make some more events if everyone likes this one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Added another sperm whale. What is it with those non-fishes and anomalies??

item.title: Staff Post - MAST - Open
item.content:encoded:
The following entry was pruned from this page:

+ show block
- hide block

Event Description: A statue became animate and began to stretch and move in various ways, apparently intended to relieve soreness. It was seen to drop the item it had been holding during this process. After approximately 45 seconds, the statue retrieved what it had dropped, returned to its original position, and returned to its inanimate state.
Date of Occurrence: 25/9/2020
Location: Metropolitan Museum of Art, New York City, NY
Follow-Up Actions Taken: Security camera footage of the event deleted. As the event occurred shortly before the museum opened for the day, there were few witnesses; amnestics were administered as required.

The idea is generally uninteresting and doesn't add much to the article, with another entry earlier in the log executing a similar idea.

item.title: New entry
item.content:encoded: added the one about the one second loss of consciousness, i just can't figure out how to make this one into a full article, so i'm putting it here until i can figure it out. enjoy or don't, i'm not your dad

item.title: New entry
item.content:encoded:
Added a new entry, which is also my first piece of writting on this website. Been lurking since 2014 but only now decided to start contributing, and I thought this place was a probably better start to try and practice writing with a clinical tone than trying to write a full-fledged SCP right out of the gate.

I also noticed that A LOT of the entries in this list have follow-up actions that essentially ammount to "footage destroyed and amnestics administered to all witnesses", so I thought it'd be interesting to have an entry where amnestics were ineffective but turns out they weren't needed at all because knowledge about the event is somewhat self-containing.

Feel free to delete it if it doesn't feel interesting enough or if the writing isn't quite up to par, idk.

item.title: Staff Post - MAST - Open
item.content:encoded:
The following entry has been pruned from this page:

+ show block
- hide block

Event Description: All bread from the brand ████ In the town of ████ Disappeared for 2 Minutes & 32 Seconds Until Reappearing 2M away from its initial location.
Date of Occurrence: 24/██/20██
Location: ████, Virginia, U.S.A
Follow-Up Actions Taken All Who witnessed have been Administered Class E amnestics.
Note: I feel bad for the people making sandwiches. -Dr ██████

The entry has several spelling and grammar errors along with the idea itself not adding anything new to the log.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Found several grammatical and spelling errors, corrected them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Added something about the charred humanoid thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: bruh

item.title: 2 New Entries
item.content:encoded: Added two entries about a mosasaurus skeleton and a giant Oriental Cockroach, respectively. Hope they're both up to snuff.

item.title: New entry and feedback
item.content:encoded: I added a new entry (the latest one about the subway train). This is my first time posting something on this site, and I'm just staring to get into writing in general so any feedback would be appreciated.

item.title: A new entry
item.content:encoded: Added a new entry, this one regarding food allergies. Props to anyone who can get the reference.

item.title: Quality
item.content:encoded:
I like how the quality of entries has been slowly getting better. For example:

Event Description: A Weather satellite with designation ███-█ orbiting over the Indian Ocean picked up a series of gravity waves erupting from a location 958 Miles to the west of Perth, Australia. The gravity waves were calculated to have fixed intervals, roughly 30 Minutes between every emission. This cycle lasted for 3 hours before ceasing. Not long after, An Earthquake was detected at the epicenter of the gravity waves and registered an M6.8 on the Richter Scale which resulted in a small 19 cm Tsunami that was observed across Perth's beaches.
Date of Occurrence: 27/05/20██
Location: Somewhere in the Indian Ocean 958 Miles west of Perth, Australia.
Follow-up Actions Taken: Scientists and Geologists who initially witnessed the the gravity waves were administered Class-A Amnestics by Foundation agents after brief questioning and all evidence relating to the gravity waves was confiscated. International news outlets declared it a normal Earthquake and tsunami. The file covering this event was moved to Site-11 pending further research.

That is an interesting event that describes something weird and inexplicable, which is also written in a scientific tone. Kudos to whoever wrote that one.

item.title: Re: Needs editing
item.content:encoded:
This isn't my event. I just wanted to say that the suggested rewrite makes so much more sense than the original, and I like it a lot more. Good idea! Whoever wrote the event should use that.

Edit: "due" in the rewrite's note should be "do"

item.title: Needs editing
item.content:encoded:
This entry could really use some fixes:

[+] Old Entry
[-] Old Entry

Event Description: At 3:30pm a giant mouth opened up in the ground and swallowed a local high school student. Then the kid burst from the closed mouth and grow bird wings they fight for around one hour then the kid dove into the mouth and they both dispersed. Twenty four the kid emerged covered in blood and walked back to the dorms. With contact with him said he remember nothing.
Date of Occurrence: 10/10/████
Location:████,████
Follow-up Actions Taken: every one in town has bean given amnestics and online cover store of movie shot. all images have been taken of the web. the kid is under watch.
NOTE: how the hell do you have to piss of for a giant mouth just to open up and try to eat you. and how blessed him with those wigs. -DR. Canary

A few things wrong with it:

  • Should be "Dr." not "DR."
  • Twenty four what? Days? Months? Parsecs?
  • Needs a comma after 3:30 pm
  • The first word of each sentence should be capitalized
[+] Rewrite Suggestion
[-] Rewrite Suggestion

Event Description: At 1530 hours, a large hole resembling a human mouth emerged from beneath a public park and swallowed a local high school student, ████████ ███. After several seconds, ███ escaped the mouth and sprouted wings similar to those of the Harpia harpyja, or Harpy Eagle. ███ then began to repeatedly attack the mouth via kicking and scratching, prompting the mouth to rotate and attempt to consume ███. After approximately ten minutes, ███ flew directly into the mouth, which sunk beneath the ground and disappeared, with nearby dirt quickly filling most of the hole.
After another twenty-four hours, ███ appeared back in their dormitory, severely bleeding and bruised and showing signs of shock. ███ claims to have no memory of the event, although was unable to account for their whereabouts during the event.
Date of Occurrence: 10/10/████-10/11/████
Location: █████ ██████, ██, USA
Follow-up Actions Taken: Local witnesses were administered Class-B Amnestics and ███ was given appropriate medical treatment. The town of █████ ██████ is to be monitored for seismic activity until 10/12/████. ███ is to be monitored for anomalous abilities until 04/12/████.
Note: What the hell did this kid do to piss off that thing? -Dr. Canary

item.title: Added two events
item.content:encoded: I just added two events, one about soccer and concrete and one about crosses. Feel free to fix any grammar mistakes, and the first one's tone is a bit weird.

item.title: Re: What?
item.content:encoded:
I think it's a "centipedes in vaginas" meme. An old meme. The event says about amnesiacing menstruating women. So, I think it's supposed to read "all menstruating women's blood, within a 15-k radius of , France, spontaneously turned into centipedes."

Honestly, I'm not a fan of memes on the SCP page. But there it is.

item.title: What?
item.content:encoded:
all shed human blood within a 15-km radius of ██████, France spontaneously turned into centipedes.

What? I'm tempted to correct it but how? How can I make this sound coherent?

item.title: Amnesiacs
item.content:encoded:
I'm not a fan of amnesiacs happening for every event. I think a fair amount should be The Foundation shrugging their shoulders and letting the public have another Fortean Event to add to their Weird US books, than hunting down everyone who saw it and mind-raping them.

+ show block
– hide block

This is an example of one of the worst offenders:

Event Description: A large, ten-centimeter thick layer of snow suddenly fell over the town of ██████, Massachusetts and coated the entire area. No clouds were visible at the time and despite sudden shifts in supported weight no buildings or structures were damaged.
Date of Occurrence: █/██/1999
Location: ██████, Massachusetts.
Follow-up Actions Taken: A sample of the snow was obtained; it was found to be mundane. All residents of the town administered Class-A amnestics. As the event occurred quickly, very little video footage was captured. However, all documentation of the event has been destroyed.

The Foundation just mind-raped an entire town because it snowed when it was not supposed to. Applying amnesiacs to a large number of people would be the opposite of what The Foundation would want to do. Because now you just created an entire town with missing time. Not to mention the practicality: Do you have MIBs all show up all over town at once and chase people down with phallic mind-wiper?

In other events, it'll read "All witnesses given Class-A amnesiacs." So, now The Foundation is tracking down people who could be anywhere in the country. We don't that everyone who saw it was a resident. How does this happen?

A lot of these events are really no big deal. The snow one I quoted, is just weird weather. It happens. Weather is weird. It'll go into a trivia book someday, that's it. People are not being subject to knowledge of dangerous paranormal activity.

Many others are like this, too. I think the writers here should take more note as to why IRL agencies cover things up and when.

1. Knowledge and access to the SCP will create unstoppable weapons.
This is why UFOs are secret. Whoever figures out UFOs figures out to travel anywhere in 100% stealth, with no weapons able to lock, at unfathomable speeds, and do impossible acrobatics. They are unstoppable weapons of war.

2. Knowledge and access to the SCP is dangerous in and of itself
This is the anti-memetic SCPs. If the public just thinks about them, they're dangerous.

3. Knowledge of the SCP Reveals Ethical Wrongs
This would be something like the government vivisecting bigfoot, or creating animal/human hybrids.

4. Knowledge and access to the SCP Threatens National Security
This would be if someone has access to an SCP that can be used as a weapon or is otherwise dangerous.

5 Knowledge of the SCP Threatens Foundation Secrecy
Seeing the SCP reveals to a person that the Foundation exists (rather than a weird creature exists). This more goes for SCP operations, than actual SCPs. Like, many Unexplained Locations where the Foundation operates, you would want to keep people away and maybe mindwipe them for seeing it. Same goes for SCP sites and Areas of Operation.

You get the idea. Seeing a ghost does not warrant an agency risking exposure to create missing time in a person or people. There has to be a good reason for the MIBs to show up. IRL, they're appearance is rare. You shoot a bigfoot, they show up. You see one, they don't care too much. Seeing a UFO, they might show, but more so to see how much you know and why the UFO showed than to mind-rape you.

Ghost tours, and paranormal trivia books are not a threat to the agency.

Those are the big reasons.


Please collapse your longposts. - nico

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh I really liked this one!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If anyone has any suggestions on what I could remove to make my latest entry shorter, I'm all ears.

+ Super-Speed Freight Train
- k thx 4 reading

Event Description: A high speed heavy goods train entered the mouth of the "Channel Tunnel" that links France and England, heading northbound for London. 45 seconds after the head of the train entered the tunnel, the entire train was within the tunnel. 2 seconds after the rear of the train had entered the tunnel, footage of the head of the train exiting the tunnel was recorded, and the remainder of the train was out of the tunnel after an additional 45 seconds. The "Channel Tunnel" is 50.45 kilometers (31.35 miles) long; as such, the train would have had to travel at a speed exceeding 3664 km/h (2401 mph) in order to travel the required distance. Security footage inside the tunnel went static during the entire event, and a speed recorder on the second engine in line indicated that the train did not exceed 153 km/h (~95 mph) at any point during the trip. The leading locomotive was found to contain [DATA EXPUNGED] inside the fuel tank, as well as six (6) large tubes sticking out of the engine block that were linked directly to the traction motors of the locomotive. (The purpose of these tubes as well as how they function is unclear.) A rail worker in charge of maintaining the engine indicated that he had been "experimenting" with the locomotive the night before the incident.
Date of Occurrence: 09/09/2019
Location: "Channel Tunnel", England/France
Follow Up Actions Taken: The driver and all witnesses were interviewed and provided Class B amnesiacs prior to release. The driver of the train did not report anything abnormal, but did say that the trip "seemed a bit quicker than normal". The rail worker is currently held at Site-11 for further questioning; the locomotive is currently stored at Site-53 pending further analysis.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Event Description: All moths in a 5 kilometer radius of a single porch light made their way towards the light and gathered there for an hour. The moths then dispersed into the surrounding area.
Date of Occurrence: ██/██/████
Location: ████████, ██
Follow-up Actions Taken: No special actions, due to lack of witnesses at time of event. The area is to be observed for more anomalous activity until 1/1/████.

If there were no witnesses…. How did we know it happened?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Apologies about that. Not really sure how to collapse images on the forums, to be honest. As for the editing comment, I'll take that into account next time for future reference.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Friendly reminder that there shouldn't be any uncollapsed images on the forums. You also shouldn't reply to your own comments, which can be done by going into Options —> Edit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Welp… I just realized why the Déjà vu was occurring: It had just reminded me of this chap from Alan Moore's magnum opus:

No uncollapsed images on the forums, please. -Zyn
hide

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: For some unknown reason, I can't seem to stop focusing on the third Event on the list (IE: The naked, glowing humanoid figure one). Dunno why, but I'm getting a really strong sense of Déjà vu from it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: An anomalous event that references “a bruh moment”? Really? Who thought this was a good idea?

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
The following log entries have been culled from the page:

show logs
hide

Event Description: On █/██/20██, at ██:██:██, Site-██ staff members that where located on the West Keter Wing, dissipated and manifested again at ██:██:██. All members of the event sung the song "Hooked on a Feeling", by Blue Swede for up to █ hours upon manifesting. All staff that dissipated where recorded to have major headaches and sore muscles. A janitor in the West Keter Wing reported all staff manifested in a room, with the similar appearance of SCP-███'s cell. From there, personnel started to dance while the music was playing for a reported ██ hours. It was claimed no one could stop dancing.
Date of Occurrence: █/██/20██
Follow-up Actions Taken: All Personnel where interviewed and where administered Class-B Amnestics and are to be placed on watch for █ Months. The West Keter Wing is to be monitored for █ Months until approval by higher authority. All SCP's that breached containment where recontained by
MTF Epsilon 11 "Nine Tailed Fox".

Reasoning: The basic idea of dancing to hooked on a feeling is fine but the way its presented is very cringey and seems more about naval-gazing at nine tailed fox than adding an interesting event

Event Description: During Researchers Tomas break, his Plague Inc game somewhat formed a connection to Agent Smith, who was as a friend hot spotting Tomas. For the next 15 minutes, all symptoms mutated the game appeared in Agent Smith. Connection was broken after Researcher Tomas accidentally disconnected from Agent Smith's hotspot after his data plan replenished and automatically reconnected to his own data plan.
Date of Occurence: 27-02-2017
Location: Site-81, Indiana, USA
Follow-up Actions Taken: Agent Tomas was quickly quarantined for further signs of illness, with no result of illness. Researcher Tomas granted $███ by Agent Smith to buy a new phone. The old phone was subsequently destroyed. No further action taken.

Reasoning: Juvenile and not really possible to take this seriously in-universe.

Event Description:

ALLOFOURGODSAREGONE?, THATSHORRIBLE

At ██:██:██ on █/█/████, all technological machines made by humans on Earth simultaneously failed to produce their intended task, then displayed the message by audio or text,
ALLOFOURGODSAREGONE?, THATSHORRIBLE.

Date of Occurrence: [??SYSTEM ERROR??]
Location: Worldwide
Follow-up Actions Taken: A Class-D was placed in false media and blamed for a worldwide hack. Class-D was given Class-A Amnestics before placed in custody by law enforcement.

Reasoning: Same as above, in addition to basically being a non-event.

I am going to be curating this page for the foreseeable future so let me know if anyone has any questions about the page and why certain entries were kept or removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Anyone else translate that Morse code one. I wonder what the Foundation is going to do about that one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It would probably go into the log of unexplained locations. Good luck ;)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If I want to write about the appearance of an island, would that go here or in the Log of Unexplained Locations?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: you're doing gods work

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: added an epic fail bruh moment because of discord friendos

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love My Hero Academia as much as the next weeb, but that particular event really has no value.

item.title: Re: 2 new posts
item.content:encoded:
Did it make your TV record sheelie?
Nuder your pets or give your laundry static cling by chance?

item.title: Re: Staff Post- Open
item.content:encoded: I wrote the "Because" one with the Beatles. Why was that one removed?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, regardless of the above, I did a little reading, which is how I even found out about the pruning to begin with. :B

item.title: Re: Staff Post- Open
item.content:encoded:
I wondered why the heck some of mine were missing. D: I mean, maybe Skypants wasn't great, but it's a good sight better than some of the really short ones, like the guy turning into wax and melting, and the dropped objects saying what sound they make. :/ Uh, let's take a look at these…

- The one about roadkill being all marsupials. I'm a little biased, but I think it's all right. I would at least like to know why it didn't cut the mustard.

- The Extra Day, I always liked that one. D: Granted, global events are a hard sell, if that's the case, I'll accept it.

- Bubbles Over Jiangsu? No love for something that's meant to be light-hearted and whimsical? Maybe I could rewrite it, it's a little… technical.

- The one about the woman in South Korea being found skinned alive. That's an image I couldn't get out of my head. What's the criteria for removal here?

- The Wendy's being replaced by a giant pile of human hair? Going for another strange and striking image there.

- And the people dancing at the Greyhound bus station. Had to go copy the text out of the history because my copy of it was janked. D: Why was this taken out?

Welcome to me caring about this page again. :| Rotten Wikidot refuses to update me on anything anymore.

item.title: Re: Staff Post- Open
item.content:encoded:
It changed it a lot more! Now it's just the Leaning Tower of Pisa simply becoming straight, and it used to be the Leaning Tower of Piza becoming straight AND turning into a stack of giant pizzas!

Also, by the way, thanks for explaining the removals!

item.title: Re: Staff Post- Open
item.content:encoded: The removal of "and turned into a stack of giant pizzas" seems more substantive than "some grammar".

item.title: Re: Staff Post- Open
item.content:encoded:
IHOB was removed because it was a low-effort pop culture joke.

The pisa change was just to correct some grammar iirc. Was the change larger than that?

item.title: Re: Staff Post- Open
item.content:encoded: What's the reason the "IHOb" one was removed? Also, why did you change the Leaning Tower of Pisa one and remove the pun ("Straight Tower of Pizzas")?

item.title: Staff Post- Open
item.content:encoded:
The pruning has been completed, with the log's size reduced from 225 entries to 120. If you feel a certain log did not deserve removal, please comment here making the case for its restoration.

The Log is now unlocked. When its size approaches the previous length, a second pruning will occur. After that, this page will be permanently locked and a second extranormal events log page will be created to continue it.

item.title: Dividing log-of-extranormal-events or making new log-of-extranormal-events.
item.content:encoded: The request is for users whose their computer is slow. Because of too many logs.

item.title: Re: Staff Post- Open
item.content:encoded: How long will this exactly take?

item.title: Staff Post- Open
item.content:encoded: After numerous requests for pruning, we're starting one now. The page has been temporarily locked in the meantime.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ok. I removed it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Out of everything, the recent entry that bugs me the most is the "teenager with falcon wings" one. Why would an SCP agent draw a bead on someone and then cap them not once, but three times, just because they're running around with a couple of outside wings strapped to their arms, resolutely failing to actually take flight? (Flapping and half running, half gliding, is about what a chicken can manage… or that Red Bull event where people launch their home-made never-gonna-work flying machines off the end of a pier…)

Sounds like someone's looking to be pulled up before a disciplinary board, with a strong likelihood of dismissal or possible demotion to D-class. Trigger happy lunatics are a threat to the foundation's secrecy and their relationship with e.g. the UIU…

item.title: Admin Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded:
Feels like it needs a katana taken to half of it, and the other half heavily proof-edited, but IDK if that would go against some kind of "don't fuck with other people's work" edict.

Staff purview applies here. I'll see about asking someone, likely Rewrite Team, about a pruning session for this log. Thank you for bringing this to our attention!

item.title: Re: 2 new posts
item.content:encoded: I don't, but your effort in making something interesting and different is appreciated :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nevermind that, wouldn't something being stolen from what is meant to be a secure testing area - with a note left by the thief, so we know it's not simply been mislaid or disintegrated - rate rather more of a response than a simple Extranormal Event log entry? At the very least you'd have strong words with the security team and be conducting a thorough review of the surveillance tapes…
